The forge has not been drawing right for weeks and nobody can figure out why
Someone brought in a piece of metalwork last week that had the whole workshop talking
The last batch of leather came in poorly cured and half the pieces had to be thrown out
A traveling tinker showed the apprentices a joining technique none of them had ever seen before
The kiln cracked during the last firing and it will take a full rebuild before anyone can use it again
The carpenter finished a set of joints so clean you cannot even see the seam where they meet
Nobody can get the alloy right since the usual ore supplier stopped sending shipments
The weaver produced a bolt of cloth so fine that a merchant offered three times the asking price on the spot
There is a commission sitting on the workbench that nobody in the shop has the skill to finish
The tools from the old master's workshop finally turned up and they are still sharper than anything we make now
Someone has been using the grinding wheel without cleaning it and the sparks nearly caught the rafters
The dye lot came out wrong and an entire run of fabric turned a color nobody ordered
The new apprentice snapped a chisel on the first day and has not been allowed near the good tools since
I watched the potter throw a piece yesterday that was perfectly centered on the first try. Took years to get there
The tanner has been complaining about the quality of hides coming in. Says they are thinner than they used to be
Someone figured out how to temper the blade steel so it holds an edge twice as long. The whole shop wants to know how
The loom broke a warp thread mid-run and the weaver had to unpick two days of work to fix it
A merchant returned a batch of nails saying they bent on the first strike. The smith was furious
The woodworker found a plank with grain so straight it could have been drawn with a ruler. Saving it for something special
They brought in a load of clay from the new dig site and it fires a completely different color than what we are used to
The bellows need replacing and nobody wants to pay for new leather when the old ones still sort of work
I heard the cooper lost three barrels to bad staves last week. The wood was green and warped during drying
The glazier has a backlog of orders stretching out past the next gathering. Everyone wants new panes at once
Someone left a piece in the annealing oven too long and it came out brittle as chalk
The fletcher has been experimenting with a new shaft wood and says the flight is noticeably straighter
An old pattern book turned up in the archive and the weavers have been crowding around it for days
The smelter ran too hot yesterday and the batch crystallized. Complete waste of ore
I noticed the mason cutting stones with a technique I have not seen before. Says he learned it on the road
The sawmill blade needs sharpening again. Third time this month. The wood coming in must have something in it
A whole shelf of pottery cracked in the kiln because someone did not wedge the clay properly
The blacksmith's hammer handle split during a swing and the head went across the shop. Nobody was hurt
They say the rope maker has been braiding a new pattern that holds three times the weight of the old one
The cobbler patched a pair of boots so well you cannot tell where the original leather ends and the repair begins
Someone tried to rush a weld and the joint failed under the first real load. Could have been dangerous
The dyer discovered a new shade by accident and now everyone wants fabric in that exact color
I found a set of old molds in the back of the foundry that are finer than anything we could make today
The workshop roof leaks right over the workbench and everything has to be covered or moved when it rains
A visiting craftsman looked at our tools and said nothing but the expression on his face said plenty
The grinding stone is wearing unevenly and pulling everything to one side. Someone needs to true it up
The charcoal supply is running low and the smith has been rationing heat all week
I heard the jeweler set a stone so precisely that the merchant thought it was a solid piece, not a setting
The apprentices have been practicing dovetail joints and the scrap pile is getting impressive
Someone mixed the wrong flux into the solder and the entire batch of fittings turned green overnight
The spindle on the great wheel cracked and the whole textile operation is waiting on a replacement part
A trader offered to sell us steel from the southern forges. Better quality but twice the price
The candle maker has been experimenting with a new wick and says the burn time is doubled
The woodcarver finished a piece for the hall that stopped people in their tracks when they saw it
I tried to sharpen my own tools last week and made them worse. There is a reason we leave it to the grinder
The workshop has been cold since the chimney cracked and nobody can work metal properly at this temperature
Someone ordered a custom piece and then changed the design three times after work had already started
The leather worker says the stitching awl from the old shop is irreplaceable. They do not make them like that anymore
A batch of rivets came in undersized and now half the fittings in the stockroom are useless
The saw keeps binding in the cut. Either the blade needs setting or the timber is too green
I noticed the mason's mortar mix has changed. It sets faster now but I am not sure it is as strong
The furnace lining needs patching before the next big melt or we risk a burn-through
Someone brought in a tool none of us could identify. Turned out to be a specialized plane for curved surfaces
The lacquer on the last batch of furniture started peeling within a week. Bad resin, apparently
They ran out of fine sand for the molds and had to crush their own. Took most of the day
The rope walk has been producing unevenly. The twist is too tight on one end and too loose on the other
An old technique for case-hardening turned up in a book and the smith has been testing it all week
The woodworker split a piece along the grain so cleanly it looked like it had been sawn
Someone used the wrong oil on the whetstone and now the whole surface needs to be resurfaced
The glass blower made a piece so thin you could read through it. Said it took six attempts to get right
A commission came in for a piece none of us have ever made before. Going to have to figure it out as we go
The tool steel has a different temper than what we usually work with. Harder, but more brittle
I counted the inventory in the workshop and we are short on rivets, tacks, and binding wire
The embroiderer has been working on a single piece for three weeks. The detail is extraordinary
Someone stored unseasoned wood next to the finished lumber and now the whole stack needs checking for warp
The forge coal has been arriving with too much slate in it. Burns cold and leaves clinker everywhere
A customer complained that the hinge work squeaks. It does not squeak. They are not oiling it properly
The old casting molds produce a cleaner pour than the new ones. Nobody understands why
I watched the smith draw out a taper so even it looked like it came from a machine. Pure skill
The joiner's glue pot cracked and spilled across the bench. Took a full day to clean and scrape it off
Someone found a seam of especially fine clay near the creek. The potters are very interested
The spinning wheel needs a new drive band and the only person who makes them is two settlements over
A whole order of cut stones arrived with the wrong dimensions. The mason is not happy about it
The brass work has been tarnishing faster than usual. Something in the air, maybe from the smelting
I heard the sword maker quenched a blade in oil instead of water and says the results are superior
The workshop apprentices are getting better. The reject pile is half what it was three months ago
Someone carved their mark into the workbench again. The master says the next person caught doing it cleans the shop for a month
The fiber for the ropes has been arriving damp and takes twice as long to process before it can be twisted
A piece of ironwork that was supposed to be decorative turned out strong enough to use structurally. Happy accident
The polishing compound ran out and the metalworkers have been making do with creek sand. It shows
I noticed the carpenter measuring everything three times before cutting. Says he learned the hard way
The tile maker is behind on the order for the hall floor. The clay keeps cracking during drying
Someone accidentally discovered that the local stone takes a better edge than the imported material
The last shipment of linen thread was full of knots. The weavers spent a whole day just picking them out
The workshop smells like burnt horn. Someone has been working antler and did not open the vents
A crack appeared in the anvil face and the smith says it has maybe a year left before it needs replacing
The etching acid spilled and ate through the worktable. Now there is a hole you could put your fist through
I helped move the new grinding wheel into the shop. Took four of us and we still nearly dropped it
The finishing nails keep splitting the trim wood. Either the nails are too thick or the wood is too dry
Someone left tools out in the rain and now half the chisels have a layer of rust that will take hours to remove
The beeswax finish on the tabletop came out perfectly smooth. Best surface work I have seen in a while
An order came in from three settlements over. Word of the quality here is apparently spreading
The wire drawing plate is worn down to the point where every gauge comes out slightly oversized
They say the wheelwright is behind on repairs because the ash supply dried up. No good bending wood available
The crucible cracked during a pour and there was molten metal on the floor. Everyone got out, but it was close
I found an old technique for waterproofing leather that uses tallow and beeswax. Works better than what we have been doing
The lathe chuck is wobbling and everything that comes off it needs extra finishing to true up
Someone delivered raw wool so full of burrs that the carders spent two days just cleaning it before they could start
The workshop floor needs new flagstones. The old ones are cracked and uneven from years of dropped tools and heat
A piece of figured wood came through the shop that was so striking the carpenter refused to cut it until he had the perfect project for it
The tinsmith soldered a patch so neatly onto the old kettle that you would never know it had a hole
I heard the mason's new apprentice set a keystone on the first attempt. The master pretended not to be impressed
The stock of good timber is dwindling and what is coming in lately has been knotty and prone to splitting
Someone oiled the leather stock with the wrong preparation and it all went stiff. Entire lot, wasted
The pattern maker carved a template so precise that every piece cut from it fits without adjustment
They finally fixed the workshop door that has been sticking since the damp season. Only took four months of complaining
The metalworker's tongs slipped during a heat and the piece landed on the floor. Ruined the temper completely
An old hand file turned up in a drawer that cuts smoother than anything the supplier sells now
The last batch of charcoal was made from green wood and it pops and spits when it burns. Dangerous near the forge
The engraver's eyesight is going and the line work shows it. Nobody has the heart to say anything
A load of iron stock came in with slag inclusions through the whole batch. Unusable for anything fine
The apprentice actually produced a decent piece yesterday. First one that did not need reworking
Someone borrowed the good calipers and returned them out of calibration. The whole measuring rack is suspect now
The varnish supplier changed their formula and the finish dries tacky instead of smooth. Entire shelf of work needs redoing
I noticed the wheelwright is using a steaming box for bends now instead of soaking. Faster, but the wood splits more
The workshop inventory has not been updated in months. Nobody knows what we actually have in stock
A commission for decorative hinges came in that requires a level of scrollwork none of the junior smiths can manage yet
